name: Frandsen Bank & Trust
description_short: Community bank with 30+ locations across Minnesota, North Dakota, and Wisconsin offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and loans since 1982.
description_long: Frandsen Bank & Trust was founded in 1982 by Dennis Frandsen and has grown to serve over 30 communities across Minnesota, North Dakota, and Wisconsin. The bank positions itself as a community-focused alternative to large national banks, emphasizing personalized service and meaningful customer relationships. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing standard deposit protections. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including personal online banking, business online banking, credit cards, investment services, wealth management, and 401(k) participant access. Their lending products span home loans (mortgages for primary residences, investment properties, and vacation homes), business loans (real estate, construction, equipment financing, and lines of credit), and personal banking services. They provide a Learning Center with calculators, budgeting templates, and banking tutorials, plus a blog covering budgeting, saving, security, and home-buying topics. Their Mobiliti app includes card management features allowing customers to turn cards on/off, limit transactions, and set travel plans. Frandsen differentiates itself through relationship-based banking with named loan officers who understand individual business operations and customize solutions accordingly. Customer testimonials highlight responsive service (PPP loan processing within days) and willingness to serve businesses that larger banks rejected. They emphasize the "small town banker" feel and personal attention that distinguishes them from national institutions. The bank serves both consumer and commercial markets with equal commitment. As a regional community bank, Frandsen is geographically limited to three Midwestern states and may lack some digital-first features of larger institutions. The website indicates a redesign coming in April, suggesting potential current limitations in online functionality. While they serve diverse customers from optometry practices to academies, their footprint and product depth may not match national banks. For customers in their service area seeking relationship-based banking, they appear competitive; for those outside their region or needing specialized products, options are limited.
services: Credit cards with Mobiliti app management
pros: 30+ physical branch locations across Minnesota, North Dakota, and Wisconsin for in-person service
Named loan officers who take time to understand individual business operations and customize solutions
Responsive business lending — PPP loans processed within days when larger banks refused
FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing
Comprehensive digital banking including Mobiliti app with card management, transaction limits, and travel planning
Mortgage specialists offering home purchase, cabin, and lake home financing
Learning Center with free calculators, budgeting templates, tutorials, and educational blog content
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Midwestern states only
New website redesign pending as of stated publication date, indicating possible current digital limitations
No mention of investment advisory depth or wealth management service specifics
Limited product information available; some service details not detailed on public website
Smaller institution may have fewer specialized lending products compared to national banks
best_for: Small business owners in Minnesota, North Dakota, or Wisconsin seeking personalized lending relationships
Consumers in their service area frustrated with impersonal service from large national banks
Home buyers and mortgage refinancers in their three-state region
Customers who value face-to-face banking relationships with consistent loan officer contact
diagnosis: Frandsen Bank & Trust is best suited for small business owners and consumers in Minnesota, North Dakota, or Wisconsin who value personal relationships with bankers and customized financial solutions over digital convenience. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to three states and potential digital service constraints, making them unsuitable for remote customers or those seeking cutting-edge fintech features.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
